items.title: Deal with US not imminent, Iran says
items.summary: The US secretary of state earlier said that an agreement could possibly come on Monday.

items.title: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
items.summary: Trump said on Saturday that an agreement would include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, without giving further details.

items.title: She was told to marry in a country which bans girls' education. So she got in a taxi and fled
items.summary: Nearly five years on from Afghanistan's school ban, young women say they have waved goodbye to their dreams.

items.title: Large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine leaves four dead and dozens injured
items.summary: A hypersonic missile, which reportedly travels over 10 times the speed of sound, was used, Russia has confirmed.

items.title: Anger grows after China's deadliest coal mining disaster in years
items.summary: On China's tightly-controlled internet, people are calling for justice and questioning how this happened.

items.title: At least 19 people taken to hospital after 'strong smell' reported at Tokyo mall
items.summary: Police say a man sprayed a substance at an ATM in the luxury Ginza 6 shopping centre.

items.title: First Hong Kong astronaut launches into space onboard Chinese mission
items.summary: The 43-year-old police officer and mother of three serves as the team's payload scientist.

items.title: Clashes as Venezuelan prisoners protest over alleged mistreatment
items.summary: The prisoners insisted their protest was peaceful but that security forces deployed to quell it opened fire.

items.title: Turkish riot police storm opposition offices after leaders ousted
items.summary: Officers forced their way into the building days after the party vowed to defy a court ruling removing its leaders.

items.title: Blast targeting train kills at least 20 in Pakistan
items.summary: Officials say the train had been transporting military personnel home for Eid when it was attacked by armed separatists.

items.title: Senegal's leadership row mounts as parliament speaker resigns
items.summary: Some speculate that El Malick Ndiaye stepped down so that ousted-PM Ousmane Sonko can take his place.

items.title: Man killed in shark attack off Australia's north-east coast
items.summary: The 39-year-old was fatally injured at a shoal off the Cassowary Coast in Queensland, apparently while spearfishing.

items.title: Suspect killed after opening fire on Secret Service near White House
items.summary: Officials say a suspect approached a checkpoint near the building and opened fire, and that a bystander was wounded.

items.title: One killed and many feared trapped under collapsed building in Philippines
items.summary: A Malaysian man has died and about 20 people are feared trapped at the site near Manila, officials say.

items.title: Ebola outbreak poses massive challenges, warns nurse
items.summary: Kate White says she is "extremely concerned about the inability to get resources" to the Democratic Republic of Congo.

items.title: Bolivian minister's convoy ambushed while overseeing roadblock clearance
items.summary: Security forces were met by resistance while attempting to clear highway barriers, amid unrest over the government's economic policies.

items.title: Currently no active leak from failing chemical tank, California officials say
items.summary: The tank, which contains a highly volatile and flammable substance, has been at risk of either a  major spillage or an explosion.

items.title: How Saudi Arabia's spending spree reached the end of the line
items.summary: MBS's Vison 2030 project seemed the stuff of science fiction. Now reality has bitten

items.title: Morocco wants tourists to visit Western Sahara. Some say it's tightening its control
items.summary: The Moroccan government wants more Western holidaymakers to visit the territory it claims to own.

items.title: Indian billionaires buy foreign companies as growth slows at home
items.summary: India Inc spent $18bn on global buyouts in 2025 and the deal value could cross $15bn in the first half of 2026.

items.title: It's like the Olympics - except steroids are allowed
items.summary: The first ever Enhanced Games are taking place this weekend in Las Vegas, with big names, big money and much controversy.

items.title: Rosenberg: Luhansk strike sparks Russian accusations and vow to retaliate
items.summary: Eighteen people were killed and 42 others injured in the strike on the town in Russian-occupied eastern Ukraine.

items.title: Moment of gas explosion at China coal mine
items.summary: At least 82 people have been killed and two are missing after a gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China on Sunday, officials say.

items.title: BBC at the site of China's worst mining disaster in more than a decade
items.summary: At least 82 people have been killed after a gas explosion at the Liushenyu mine in China.

items.title: Watch: Pitches, PlayStations and protein ice cream - A look inside Brazil's World Cup base camp
items.summary: BBC's Brandon Livesay toured the Columbia Park Training Facility in Morris Township, New Jersey that will host the team in the coming weeks.

items.title: Moment SpaceX rocket explodes in the Indian Ocean after splashdown
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX has successfully launched its Starship V3 rocket, which landed in a planned fiery explosion on Friday.
